title: Add graceful shutdown with preStop hook and startup probe
summary: Addresses external review W-001 by adding a Chainsaw E2E test directory tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/ with chainsaw-test.yaml and 00-keystone-cr.yaml. The test applies a minimal Keystone CR, waits for Ready=True, then asserts the Deployment spec contains terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30, preStop exec sleep 5, and startupProbe httpGet /v3:5000 with failureThreshold 30 and periodSeconds 10. All files follow established conventions exactly: SPDX headers, unique CR/database names, numbered step comments, 5m assert timeout, catch blocks with diagnostic scripts. Metadata files (.planwerk/progress, reviews, review_patterns) are correctly updated.

tests_checklist.item: E2E test exists for graceful-shutdown feature (W-001)
Test asserts preStop lifecycle hook with sleep 5
Test asserts startupProbe httpGet /v3:5000 failureThreshold 30 periodSeconds 10
Test waits for Ready=True before asserting Deployment spec
Catch blocks provide diagnostic output on failure
Unique CR name (keystone-graceful-shutdown) prevents parallel test collision
Unique database name (keystone_graceful_shutdown) prevents cross-test interference

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows Chainsaw E2E test suite structure pattern
SPDX Apache-2.0 headers on all new files
Descriptive comment block with feature ID and requirement IDs
Step comments use established '# -- Step N: Description --' format

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in test fixtures

architecture_checklist.item: CR fixture is minimal (only required fields)
No scope creep beyond external review feedback

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unnecessary assertions beyond the three new fields

fail_fast_checklist.item: Ready gate (Step 2) prevents spec assertions on unready Deployment

defensive_checklist.item: Catch blocks handle diagnostic commands defensively (2>/dev/null, || true)
